bullet_points: Load the close_prices.csv dataset.
Filter for the 9 stable sector ETFs (XLK, XLF, XLV, XLE, XLI, XLY, XLP, XLU, XLB) and the ^VIX index and ^GSPC index.
Calculate daily log-returns for the 9 selected sector ETFs and ^GSPC.
Load and save raw ^VIX price levels alongside log-returns.
Handle missing values by dropping rows where any of the 9 sector ETFs have missing data to ensure a balanced panel.
Save the cleaned log-returns, raw VIX levels, and GSPC returns to a file for subsequent steps.
Load the cleaned data from Step 1.
Calculate the 21-day rolling realized volatility for each of the 9 sector ETFs, annualizing by multiplying the standard deviation of log-returns by sqrt(252).
Compute the Cross-Sectional Standard Deviation (CSSD) of these 9 annualized realized volatility series for each day t to define the SVD metric.
Apply Z-score normalization to the SVD series using a 252-day rolling window.
Print descriptive statistics (mean, std, min, max) for both raw and normalized SVD series.
Save the SVD series and the rolling realized volatility data.
Load the data from previous steps.
Transform the ^VIX series into log-returns.
Implement a Hidden Markov Model (HMM) with three states fitted on the full ^VIX log-return series.
Map HMM states to Low/Moderate/High regimes based on the mean ^VIX level within each state.
Perform ADF tests on both raw and normalized SVD series and print full test results (ADF statistic, p-value, lags) to the console.
Generate lagged features of the SVD (SVD_t-1, SVD_t-5, SVD_t-21) and calculate the 21-day rolling average pairwise correlation of the 9 sector ETFs.
Save the regime labels and engineered features.
sub_tasks.sub_task: VIX Regime Definition and Feature Engineering

bullet_points: Load the features and regime labels.
Estimate the regression of delta ln(VIX)_t+k against lagged SVD, ^GSPC returns, and interaction terms using Newey-West standard errors for k=5 and k=21.
Print full regression summary tables (coefficients, standard errors, t-stats, p-values, R-squared) for both horizons.
Perform logistic regression to predict the probability of transitioning into a High volatility regime using lagged SVD.
Conduct leave-one-out cross-validation and print the distribution (mean, std, min, max) of AUROC scores.
Save all regression coefficients and model performance metrics.
Load the saved results and metrics.
Generate a consolidated multi-panel figure showing ^VIX levels with regime color-coding, the SVD series, and the average pairwise correlation on a shared time axis.
Generate plots for model performance (AUROC/Precision-Recall curves).
Save all plots as high-resolution PNG files.
Synthesize the quantitative results, statistical test outputs, and visual evidence generated in previous steps.
Interpret the predictive power of SVD regarding ^VIX regime transitions.
Discuss the implications of the findings for market risk management and sector decoupling.
Write the full Results section of the research report, referencing the generated plots and key statistics.
